immutable_core.semantic_intent: ICU baseline body mass index snapshot
immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-ICU-stay baseline BMI snapshot derived only from approved retained ICU-baseline weight in kilograms and approved retained subject-level height in centimeters, using weight_kg / (height_m ^ 2), rather than a raw BMI source value, a patient-level stable BMI, a hospital-admission baseline BMI, or a first-day average BMI.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_bmi_icu_baseline must preserve ICU-stay baseline meaning, kg/m^2 representation, formula, upstream weight/height dependency, and explicit no-source-row behavior. It must not silently shift to hospital-admission baseline BMI, subject-level BMI, raw source BMI, or averaged BMI.

summary_contract.anchor_family: ICU admission anchor inherited from std_weight_icu_baseline
summary_contract.window_start_rule: inherit the selected baseline weight window from std_weight_icu_baseline
summary_contract.window_end_rule: inherit the selected baseline weight window from std_weight_icu_baseline
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: a qualifying row is a retained std_weight_icu_baseline row with a non-null approved std_height row for the same subject_id
summary_contract.selection_rule: selection is inherited from std_weight_icu_baseline; no new BMI-specific source-row selection is introduced
summary_contract.aggregation_rule: no numeric aggregation; compute BMI from the selected baseline weight and subject-level height
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: tie-break behavior is inherited from std_weight_icu_baseline for weight selection; std_height provides one retained height row per subject under its own approved logic
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not retain an ICU stay row when either the approved ICU-baseline weight or approved subject-level height is unavailable
summary_contract.partial_window_action: partial window behavior is inherited from std_weight_icu_baseline

canonical_representation.range_role_note: Values outside the outlier flag range are retained with is_bmi_outlier_by_contract rather than silently dropped. The hard range is intentionally wider than the review-flag range so retained flagged rows do not contradict the machine-readable representation.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external references as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.public_anchor_status: BMI is a derived body-size metric; external BMI concepts and CDC formula references are useful anchors, but the ICU-baseline stay snapshot semantics are governed by this internal upstream-dependency and anchor contract.

candidate_external_refs.label: BMI = weight / height squared using metric units

external_alignment.note: No single external code is treated as an exact replacement for this ICU-baseline derived variable because the standard identity includes target grain, upstream dependencies, no-source-row behavior, and retained outlier-flag behavior.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_bmi_icu_baseline from ICU-baseline BMI to hospital-admission baseline BMI
changing from derived BMI using approved upstream assets to raw source BMI
turning the variable into first-day mean BMI or patient-level stable BMI
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same ICU-baseline meaning
changing the display precision or review range after explicit review without changing the formula or target grain

allowed_database_specific_variation: local upstream weight and height source tables used before approved retained assets are created
local identifiers carried as provenance fields
local height and weight source-priority rules when explicitly reviewed in upstream assets
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing raw database BMI as std_bmi_icu_baseline without deriving from approved weight and height assets
silently dropping outlier-flagged BMI rows instead of retaining the explicit flag
using grouped/proxy weight or height as if it were exact measured BMI without a distinct reviewed identity

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_bmi_icu_baseline outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_bmi_icu_baseline outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 is not approved for this exact ICU-baseline BMI identity in the current public MVP surface.
